title: Limits and Continuity
description: Introduction Before we can discuss derivatives (the engine of optimization) or integrals (the foundation of probability), we must understand the bedrock they stand on: limits. Calculus is the mathematics of change. But change happens at an instant, and measuring something "at an instant" involves dividing by zero ( О” рқ‘Ұ 0 0 О”y вҖӢ ). To bypass this mathematical impossibility, we use limits: asking not "what is the value at this point," but "what value do we approach as we get infinitely close?" Why Limits Matter for ML Derivatives: The definition of derivative is a limit: рқ‘“ вҖІ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = lim вҒЎ в„Һ вҶ’ 0 рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ + в„Һ ) вҲ’ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) в„Һ f вҖІ (x)=lim hвҶ’0 вҖӢ h f(x+h)вҲ’f(x) вҖӢ Gradient Descent: Requires continuous, differentiable functions. Limits ensure this. Activation Functions: Understanding where functions break (discontinuities) explains ReLU vs Sigmoid choices. Convergence: Training loops converge when loss approaches a limit. What is a Limit? A limit describes the value a function approaches as the input approaches some value. The function doesn't need to actually reach that value, it just needs to get arbitrarily close. Classic Example Consider рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқ‘Ҙ 2 вҲ’ 1 рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 f(x)= xвҲ’1 x 2 вҲ’1 вҖӢ . If we plug in x = 1, we get 0 0 0 0 вҖӢ , which is undefined. But we can factor: рқ‘Ҙ 2 вҲ’ 1 рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 = ( рқ‘Ҙ + 1 ) ( рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 ) рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 = рқ‘Ҙ + 1 xвҲ’1 x 2 вҲ’1 вҖӢ = xвҲ’1 (x+1)(xвҲ’1) вҖӢ =x+1 for x != 1. So as x approaches 1, f(x) approaches 2. The limit exists even though f(1) is undefined! Left-hand Limit lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ вҲ’ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) lim xвҶ’c вҲ’ вҖӢ f(x) Approaching c from values less than c (from the left on number line). Right-hand Limit lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ + рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) lim xвҶ’c + вҖӢ f(x) Approaching c from values greater than c (from the right). Limit Exists If and Only If The limit lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқҗҝ lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ f(x)=L exists if and only if both one-sided limits exist AND are equal: lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ вҲ’ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ + рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқҗҝ lim xвҶ’c вҲ’ вҖӢ f(x)=lim xвҶ’c + вҖӢ f(x)=L Interactive: Approaching a Limit Watch how both sides approach the same value as we get closer to the point. The function has a "hole" at x = 1, but the limit still exists! Approaching the Limit Visualize lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ 1 рқ‘Ҙ 2 вҲ’ 1 рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 = 2 lim xвҶ’1 вҖӢ xвҲ’1 x 2 вҲ’1 вҖӢ =2. The hole at x=1 doesn't stop the limit! Zoom In 10x Animate Approach x=1 y=2 DISTANCE VALUES TABLE Left Limit рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ 1 вҲ’ xвҶ’1 вҲ’ x -0.5000 f(x) 0.5000 Right Limit рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ 1 + xвҶ’1 + x 2.5000 f(x) 3.5000 Limit Exists! Notice that as x gets closer to 1 from BOTH sides, f(x) gets closer to 2. Even though f(1) is undefined (hole), we say lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ 1 рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = 2 lim xвҶ’1 вҖӢ f(x)=2. The Epsilon-Delta Definition The formal definition of a limit is often considered one of the hardest concepts in Calculus 1. But it's actually just a way of describing a guarantee of precision. The Manufacturing Analogy Imagine you are manufacturing a high-precision piston (input рқ‘Ҙ x) that must fit into a cylinder (output рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) f(x)). 1. The Goal (L): The cylinder has a perfect target width, say 10cm. 2. The Tolerance ( рқң– Пө): The customer says, "The width must be within 0.01cm of the target." This is your error margin. 3. The Input Precision ( рқӣҝ Оҙ): You ask, "How precise must my piston mold be?" Maybe if the mold is within 0.005cm of the target size, the final piston will be within the customer's tolerance. The Limit Exists If: No matter how strict the customer's tolerance ( рқң– Пө) is, you can always find a high-enough precision for your machine ( рқӣҝ Оҙ) to satisfy it. Formal Definition We say lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқҗҝ lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ f(x)=L if: For every рқң– > 0 Пө>0 (the challenge), there exists a рқӣҝ > 0 Оҙ>0 (the response) such that: 0 < вҲЈ рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ рқ‘җ вҲЈ < рқӣҝ вҹ№ вҲЈ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) вҲ’ рқҗҝ вҲЈ < рқң– 0<вҲЈxвҲ’cвҲЈ<Оҙвҹ№вҲЈf(x)вҲ’LвҲЈ<Пө Translation: If the input рқ‘Ҙ x is within рқӣҝ Оҙ of рқ‘җ c, then the output рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) f(x) is guaranteed to be within рқң– Пө of рқҗҝ L. Limit Laws These rules let us compute limits of complex expressions from simpler ones. If lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқҗҝ lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ f(x)=L and lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘” ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқ‘Җ lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ g(x)=M: Sum/Difference lim вҒЎ [ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) Вұ рқ‘” ( рқ‘Ҙ ) ] = рқҗҝ Вұ рқ‘Җ lim[f(x)Вұg(x)]=LВұM Product lim вҒЎ [ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) вӢ… рқ‘” ( рқ‘Ҙ ) ] = рқҗҝ вӢ… рқ‘Җ lim[f(x)вӢ…g(x)]=LвӢ…M Quotient lim вҒЎ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) рқ‘” ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқҗҝ рқ‘Җ lim g(x) f(x) вҖӢ = M L вҖӢ (if M != 0) Scalar Multiple lim вҒЎ [ рқ‘ҳ вӢ… рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) ] = рқ‘ҳ вӢ… рқҗҝ lim[kвӢ…f(x)]=kвӢ…L Power lim вҒЎ [ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) ] рқ‘ӣ = рқҗҝ рқ‘ӣ lim[f(x)] n =L n Composition lim вҒЎ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘” ( рқ‘Ҙ ) ) = рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Җ ) limf(g(x))=f(M)(if f continuous at M) L'Hopital's Rule When direct substitution gives an indeterminate form like 0 0 0 0 вҖӢ or вҲһ вҲһ вҲһ вҲһ вҖӢ , L'Hopital's Rule provides a way forward. L'Hopital's Rule If lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) рқ‘” ( рқ‘Ҙ ) lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ g(x) f(x) вҖӢ gives 0 0 0 0 вҖӢ or Вұ вҲһ Вұ вҲһ ВұвҲһ ВұвҲһ вҖӢ , then: lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) рқ‘” ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ вҖІ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) рқ‘” вҖІ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ g(x) f(x) вҖӢ =lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ g вҖІ (x) f вҖІ (x) вҖӢ provided the limit on the right exists (or is infinity). Example Find lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ 0 sin вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ рқ‘Ҙ lim xвҶ’0 вҖӢ x sinx вҖӢ : Direct substitution: sin вҒЎ 0 0 = 0 0 0 sin0 вҖӢ = 0 0 вҖӢ (indeterminate) Apply L'Hopital: lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ 0 cos вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ 1 = cos вҒЎ 0 1 = 1 lim xвҶ’0 вҖӢ 1 cosx вҖӢ = 1 cos0 вҖӢ =1 This limit (sin x / x = 1 as x goes to 0) is fundamental in deriving the derivative of sine. Caution Only apply L'Hopital when you have an indeterminate form! Applying it to 1 0 0 1 вҖӢ (which is just undefined, not indeterminate) gives wrong answers. Continuity Intuitively, a function is continuous if you can draw it without lifting your pen. Formally, continuity requires three conditions at a point c: Three Conditions for Continuity at c 1 рқ‘“ ( рқ‘җ ) f(c) is defined No holes at the point. 2 lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ f(x) exists Left and right limits agree. 3 lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқ‘“ ( рқ‘җ ) lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ f(x)=f(c) The limit equals the function value. Important Properties Polynomials are continuous everywhere. Rational functions are continuous except where denominator = 0. Exponential, logarithmic, and trig functions are continuous on their domains. Sums, products, and compositions of continuous functions are continuous. Types of Discontinuity Understanding where and how functions "break" is crucial for choosing activation functions and understanding gradient flow. Types of Discontinuity Select a type to see how the limit behaves and why it matters for ML. Removable Jump Infinite x=c Removable Discontinuity рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқ‘Ҙ 2 + рқ‘Ҙ рқ‘Ҙ f(x)= x x 2 +x вҖӢ lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқҗҝ lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ f(x)=L The limit exists (left = right), but the function is undefined at the point (or defined differently). WHY IT MATTERS FOR ML Removable discontinuities are trivialвҖ”we just 'fill the hole'. For example, defining 0/0 or 0*log(0) as 0 in entropy calculations. Removable Limit exists but f(c) is undefined or wrong. Can be "fixed" by redefining f(c) = L. Example: (x^2-1)/(x-1) at x=1 Jump Left and right limits both exist but differ. Function "jumps." Example: Heaviside step function Infinite Function approaches infinity (vertical asymptote). Limit DNE. Example: 1/x at x=0 Continuity vs Differentiability This distinction is critical for understanding activation functions in deep learning. The Hierarchy All Functions includes Continuous includes Differentiable Differentiable implies Continuous If f'(c) exists, then f must be continuous at c. You can't have a derivative at a gap or jump. Continuous does NOT imply Differentiable A function can be continuous but have a "corner" where the derivative is undefined. The Classic Example: |x| The absolute value function f(x) = |x| is continuous everywhere. But at x = 0: Left derivative: lim вҒЎ в„Һ вҶ’ 0 вҲ’ вҲЈ 0 + в„Һ вҲЈ вҲ’ вҲЈ 0 вҲЈ в„Һ = lim вҒЎ в„Һ вҶ’ 0 вҲ’ вҲ’ в„Һ в„Һ = вҲ’ 1 lim hвҶ’0 вҲ’ вҖӢ h вҲЈ0+hвҲЈвҲ’вҲЈ0вҲЈ вҖӢ =lim hвҶ’0 вҲ’ вҖӢ h вҲ’h вҖӢ =вҲ’1 Right derivative: lim вҒЎ в„Һ вҶ’ 0 + вҲЈ 0 + в„Һ вҲЈ вҲ’ вҲЈ 0 вҲЈ в„Һ = lim вҒЎ в„Һ вҶ’ 0 + в„Һ в„Һ = + 1 lim hвҶ’0 + вҖӢ h вҲЈ0+hвҲЈвҲ’вҲЈ0вҲЈ вҖӢ =lim hвҶ’0 + вҖӢ h h вҖӢ =+1 Since -1 != +1, the derivative at 0 does not exist. The function has a "corner." ML Applications The Death of the Perceptron Early neural networks used the step function: output 0 if input is negative, 1 otherwise. Problem: It has a jump discontinuity at 0. The derivative is 0 everywhere else, so gradients cannot flow. Backpropagation fails completely. This is why we moved to smooth activations like Sigmoid. ReLU: The Practical Compromise ReLU: рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = max вҒЎ ( 0 , рқ‘Ҙ ) f(x)=max(0,x). It is continuous everywhere but has a corner at x = 0 (not differentiable there). Solution: We use subgradients. We arbitrarily define f'(0) = 0 or 1. Since the probability of x being exactly 0.0000... is negligible, this works perfectly in practice. Softmax and Numerical Stability Softmax: рқңҺ ( рқ‘§ рқ‘– ) = рқ‘’ рқ‘§ рқ‘– вҲ‘ рқ‘— рқ‘’ рқ‘§ рқ‘— Пғ(z i вҖӢ )= вҲ‘ j вҖӢ e z j вҖӢ e z i вҖӢ вҖӢ . When рқ‘§ рқ‘– z i вҖӢ gets large, рқ‘’ рқ‘§ рқ‘– e z i вҖӢ can overflow to infinity. We use the trick of subtracting max(z) from all inputs, which doesn't change the output but keeps values bounded. This is a practical application of limit behavior. Loss Function Continuity MSE Loss ( рқ‘Ұ вҲ’ рқ‘Ұ ^ ) 2 (yвҲ’ y ^ вҖӢ ) 2 is continuous and differentiable everywhere, making gradient descent smooth. Cross-entropy loss is continuous but has a singularity as predictions approach 0 or 1, which is why we clip probabilities away from exactly 0/1. Next: Partial Derivatives

Introduction Before we can discuss derivatives (the engine of optimization) or integrals (the foundation of probability), we must understand the bedrock they stand on: limits. Calculus is the mathematics of change. But change happens at an instant, and measuring something "at an instant" involves dividing by zero ( О” рқ‘Ұ 0 0 О”y вҖӢ ). To bypass this mathematical impossibility, we use limits: asking not "what is the value at this point," but "what value do we approach as we get infinitely close?" Why Limits Matter for ML Derivatives: The definition of derivative is a limit: рқ‘“ вҖІ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = lim вҒЎ в„Һ вҶ’ 0 рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ + в„Һ ) вҲ’ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) в„Һ f вҖІ (x)=lim hвҶ’0 вҖӢ h f(x+h)вҲ’f(x) вҖӢ Gradient Descent: Requires continuous, differentiable functions. Limits ensure this. Activation Functions: Understanding where functions break (discontinuities) explains ReLU vs Sigmoid choices. Convergence: Training loops converge when loss approaches a limit. What is a Limit? A limit describes the value a function approaches as the input approaches some value. The function doesn't need to actually reach that value, it just needs to get arbitrarily close. Classic Example Consider рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқ‘Ҙ 2 вҲ’ 1 рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 f(x)= xвҲ’1 x 2 вҲ’1 вҖӢ . If we plug in x = 1, we get 0 0 0 0 вҖӢ , which is undefined. But we can factor: рқ‘Ҙ 2 вҲ’ 1 рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 = ( рқ‘Ҙ + 1 ) ( рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 ) рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 = рқ‘Ҙ + 1 xвҲ’1 x 2 вҲ’1 вҖӢ = xвҲ’1 (x+1)(xвҲ’1) вҖӢ =x+1 for x != 1. So as x approaches 1, f(x) approaches 2. The limit exists even though f(1) is undefined! Left-hand Limit lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ вҲ’ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) lim xвҶ’c вҲ’ вҖӢ f(x) Approaching c from values less than c (from the left on number line). Right-hand Limit lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ + рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) lim xвҶ’c + вҖӢ f(x) Approaching c from values greater than c (from the right). Limit Exists If and Only If The limit lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқҗҝ lim xвҶ’c вҖӢ f(x)=L exists if and only if both one-sided limits exist AND are equal: lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ вҲ’ рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ рқ‘җ + рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = рқҗҝ lim xвҶ’c вҲ’ вҖӢ f(x)=lim xвҶ’c + вҖӢ f(x)=L Interactive: Approaching a Limit Watch how both sides approach the same value as we get closer to the point. The function has a "hole" at x = 1, but the limit still exists! Approaching the Limit Visualize lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ 1 рқ‘Ҙ 2 вҲ’ 1 рқ‘Ҙ вҲ’ 1 = 2 lim xвҶ’1 вҖӢ xвҲ’1 x 2 вҲ’1 вҖӢ =2. The hole at x=1 doesn't stop the limit! Notice that as x gets closer to 1 from BOTH sides, f(x) gets closer to 2. Even though f(1) is undefined (hole), we say lim вҒЎ рқ‘Ҙ вҶ’ 1 рқ‘“ ( рқ‘Ҙ ) = 2 lim xвҶ’1 вҖӢ f(x)=
